title: Three hand-lettered header styles for journal pages
sources: Edward Johnston, Writing and Illuminating and Lettering (1906) — layouts and headings
notes: Three header treatments: (1) simple Foundational lettering in a ruled line, (2) a simple drop-shadow block letter pencilled and inked, (3) a watercolour wash title with ink outline. Each taught step by step. No brush-lettering — the journal headers here use a broad-edge nib or a pencil layout, not a modern brush-lettering pen.
